description:
A Prompty is a markdown file format for LLM prompts. The frontmatter defines
structured metadata including model configuration, input/output schemas, tools,
and template settings. The markdown body becomes the instructions.

This is the single root type for the Prompty schema — there is no abstract base
class or kind discriminator. A .prompty file always produces a Prompty instance.

Runtime loaders may resolve frontmatter references such as `${env:VAR}` and
`${file:relative/path}`. File references must be treated as a host-controlled
capability: by default they are scoped to the containing .prompty file's
directory tree after canonicalization, and any additional allowed roots must
be supplied by the host application's load options rather than frontmatter.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Human-readable name of the prompt

properties.name: displayName
properties.description: Display name for UI purposes

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the prompt's purpose

sample.description: A basic prompt that uses the GPT-3 chat API to answer questions

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: Additional metadata including authors, tags, and other arbitrary properties

properties.name: inputs
properties.description: Input parameters that participate in template rendering

question.default: What is the meaning of life?

inputs.name: question
inputs.default: What is the meaning of life?

type.description:
Represents a single property.

- This model defines the structure of properties that can be used in prompts,
including their type, description, whether they are required, and other attributes.
- It allows for the definition of dynamic inputs that can be filled with data
and processed to generate prompts for AI models.

coercions.description: Simple construction with just a kind of integer

coercions.title: input
coercions.description: Simple construction with just a kind of string

This extends the base Property model to represent an array of items.

properties.description: The type of items contained in the array

childTypes.description:
Represents a JSON Schema union property.

Use `oneOf` when exactly one branch must match, or `anyOf` when one or more
branches may match. Exactly one composition field MUST be provided with at
least one branch; `oneOf` and `anyOf` MUST NOT both be populated. The
alternatives are full Prompty properties so unions remain portable across
generated runtimes.

properties.name: oneOf
properties.description: Alternative property schemas where exactly one branch must match

properties.description: Whether the property is required

properties.description: Whether the property also accepts a JSON null value

properties.description: Example value used for either initialization or tooling

properties.name: enumValues
properties.description: Allowed enumeration values for the property

properties.name: anyOf
properties.description: Alternative property schemas where one or more branches may match

properties.name: model
properties.description: AI model configuration

type.description:
Model for defining the structure and behavior of AI agents.
This model includes properties for specifying the model's provider, connection details, and various options.
It allows for flexible configuration of AI models to suit different use cases and requirements.

coercions.title: model
coercions.description: Simple construction with just an id

properties.name: id
properties.description: The unique identifier of the model - can be used as the single property shorthand

properties.name: provider
properties.description: The provider of the model (e.g., 'openai', 'foundry', 'anthropic')

properties.name: apiType
properties.description: The type of API to use for the model (e.g., 'chat', 'response', etc.)

properties.name: connection
properties.description: The connection configuration for the model

type.description:
Connection configuration for AI agents.
`provider`, `kind`, and `endpoint` are required properties here,
but this section can accept additional via options.

childTypes.description: Connection configuration for AI services using named connections.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The Authentication kind for the AI service (e.g., 'key' for API key, 'oauth' for OAuth tokens)

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the connection

properties.name: target
properties.description: The target resource or service that this connection refers to

childTypes.description: Connection configuration for AI services using named connections.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The Authentication kind for the AI service (e.g., 'key' for API key, 'oauth' for OAuth tokens)

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the connection

properties.name: endpoint
properties.description: The endpoint URL for the AI service

childTypes.description: Connection configuration for AI services using API keys.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The Authentication kind for the AI service (e.g., 'key' for API key, 'oauth' for OAuth tokens)

properties.name: endpoint
properties.description: The endpoint URL for the AI service

properties.name: apiKey
properties.description: The API key for authenticating with the AI service

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The Authentication kind for the AI service (e.g., 'key' for API key, 'oauth' for OAuth tokens)

properties.name: endpoint
properties.description: The endpoint for authenticating with the AI service

childTypes.description:
Connection configuration using OAuth 2.0 client credentials.
Useful for tools and services that require OAuth authentication,
such as MCP servers, OpenAPI endpoints, or other REST APIs.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The connection kind for OAuth authentication

properties.name: endpoint
properties.description: The endpoint URL for the service

properties.name: clientId
properties.description: The OAuth client ID

properties.name: clientSecret
properties.description: The OAuth client secret

properties.name: tokenUrl
properties.description: The OAuth token endpoint URL

properties.name: scopes
properties.description: OAuth scopes to request

childTypes.description:
Connection configuration for Microsoft Foundry projects.
Provides project-scoped access to models, tools, and services
via Entra ID (DefaultAzureCredential) authentication.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The connection kind for Foundry project access

properties.name: endpoint
properties.description: The Foundry project endpoint URL

properties.name: name
properties.description: The named connection within the Foundry project

properties.name: connectionType
properties.description: The connection type within the Foundry project (e.g., 'model', 'index', 'storage')

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The Authentication kind for the AI service (e.g., 'key' for API key, 'oauth' for OAuth tokens)

properties.name: authenticationMode
properties.description: The authority level for the connection, indicating under whose authority the connection is made (e.g., 'user', 'agent', 'system')

properties.name: usageDescription
properties.description: The usage description for the connection, providing context on how this connection will be used

sample.usageDescription: This will allow the agent to respond to an email on your behalf

properties.name: options
properties.description: Additional options for the model

type.description: Options for configuring the behavior of the AI model.

properties.name: frequencyPenalty
properties.description: The frequency penalty to apply to the model's output

properties.name: maxOutputTokens
properties.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the output

properties.name: presencePenalty
properties.description: The presence penalty to apply to the model's output

properties.name: seed
properties.description: A random seed for deterministic output

properties.name: temperature
properties.description: The temperature to use for sampling

properties.name: topK
properties.description: The top-K sampling value

properties.name: topP
properties.description: The top-P sampling value

properties.name: stopSequences
properties.description: Stop sequences to end generation

properties.name: allowMultipleToolCalls
properties.description: Whether to allow multiple tool calls in a single response

properties.name: additionalProperties
properties.description: Additional custom properties for model options

properties.name: tools
properties.description: Tools available for extended functionality

tools.name: getCurrentWeather
tools.description: Get the current weather in a given location

location.description: The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA

unit.description: The unit of temperature, e.g. Celsius or Fahrenheit

getCurrentWeather.description: Get the current weather in a given location

location.description: The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA

unit.description: The unit of temperature, e.g. Celsius or Fahrenheit

type.description: Represents a tool that can be used in prompts.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind identifier for function tools

properties.name: parameters
properties.description: Parameters accepted by the function tool

question.default: What is the meaning of life?

parameters.name: question
parameters.default: What is the meaning of life?

properties.name: strict
properties.description: Indicates whether the function tool enforces strict validation on its parameters

childTypes.description:
Represents a generic server tool that runs on a server
This tool kind is designed for operations that require server-side execution
It may include features such as authentication, data storage, and long-running processes
This tool kind is ideal for tasks that involve complex computations or access to secure resources
Server tools can be used to offload heavy processing from client applications

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind identifier for server tools. This is a wildcard and can represent any server tool type not explicitly defined.

properties.name: connection
properties.description: Connection configuration for the server tool

properties.name: options
properties.description: Configuration options for the server tool

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind identifier for MCP tools

properties.name: connection
properties.description: The connection configuration for the MCP tool

properties.name: serverName
properties.description: The server name of the MCP tool

properties.name: serverDescription
properties.description: The description of the MCP tool

sample.serverDescription: This tool allows access to MCP services.

properties.name: approvalMode
properties.description: The approval mode for the MCP tool

type.description:
The approval mode for MCP server tools.
When kind is "specify", use alwaysRequireApprovalTools and neverRequireApprovalTools
to control per-tool approval. For "always" and "never", those fields are ignored.

coercions.title: kind
coercions.description: Mcp Approval Mode

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The approval mode: 'always', 'never', or 'specify'

properties.name: alwaysRequireApprovalTools
properties.description: List of tools that always require approval (only used when kind is 'specify')

properties.name: neverRequireApprovalTools
properties.description: List of tools that never require approval (only used when kind is 'specify')

properties.name: allowedTools
properties.description: List of allowed operations or resources for the MCP tool

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind identifier for OpenAPI tools

properties.name: connection
properties.description: The connection configuration for the OpenAPI tool

properties.name: specification
properties.description: The full OpenAPI specification

childTypes.description:
A tool that references another .prompty file to be invoked as a tool.

The child prompty is executed as a single prompt invocation. Nested agent
loops are intentionally not started from PromptyTool.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind identifier for prompty tools

properties.name: path
properties.description: Path to the child .prompty file, relative to the parent

properties.name: mode
properties.description: Execution mode. Only 'single' is supported; nested agent loops are not started from PromptyTool.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name of the tool. If a function tool, this is the function name, otherwise it is the type

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind identifier for the tool

properties.name: description
properties.description: A short description of the tool for metadata purposes

properties.name: bindings
properties.description: Tool argument bindings to input properties

type.description: Represents a binding between an input property and a tool parameter.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name of the binding

properties.name: input
properties.description: The input property that will be bound to the tool parameter argument

properties.name: template
properties.description: Template configuration for prompt rendering

type.description:
Template model for defining prompt templates.

This model specifies the rendering engine used for slot filling prompts,
the parser used to process the rendered template into API-compatible format,
and additional options for the template engine.

It allows for the creation of reusable templates that can be filled with dynamic data
and processed to generate prompts for AI models.

properties.name: format
properties.description: Template rendering engine used for slot filling prompts (e.g., mustache, jinja2)

coercions.title: format
coercions.description: Simple construction with just a "kind" string

properties.name: kind
properties.description: Template rendering engine used for slot filling prompts (e.g., mustache, jinja2)

properties.name: strict
properties.description: Whether the template can emit structural text for parsing output

properties.name: options
properties.description: Options for the template engine

properties.name: parser
properties.description: Parser used to process the rendered template into API-compatible format

coercions.title: parser
coercions.description: Simple construction with just a "kind" string

properties.name: kind
properties.description: Parser used to process the rendered template into API-compatible format

properties.name: options
properties.description: Options for the parser

properties.name: instructions
properties.description: Clear directions on what the prompt should do. In .prompty files, this comes from the markdown body.

sample.instructions:
system:
You are an AI assistant who helps people find information.
As the assistant, you answer questions briefly, succinctly,
and in a personable manner using markdown and even add some 
personal flair with appropriate emojis.

# Customer
You are helping {{firstName}} {{lastName}} to find answers to 
their questions. Use their name to address them in your responses.
user:
{{question}}
